name: Data Reconciliation Controls and Checks
description: A structured validation framework ensuring data transmission success across source and target systems. It mandates record count matching, string/checksum field verification, and strict preservation of data formats and types. Invalid data is automatically flagged to trigger corrective actions.
context: Data Reconciliation Controls and Checks
The number of customer records with ‘Country of  Origin’ matches between source and target
the format and data type of   ‘Country of origin’ field has not undergone any changes

name: Interface Level Key Controls
description: A set of operational safeguards applied to all data elements across system interfaces. It requires built-in job controls, automated halt triggers for threshold-exceeding mismatches, and comprehensive logging for issue resolution and audit trails.
context: Interface Level  Key Controls (applicable for all CDEs)
the interface  must halt data loading and trigger a alert
A detailed log should be maintained for every loading  operation with issues separately highlighted for resolution

name: Country of Origin Data Maintenance
description: A critical data element (CDE) control requiring strict state preservation from initial system entry through final utilization. The framework explicitly prohibits any modification, alteration, or update to this data element across all intermediary systems and processing stages.
context: The ‘Country of Origin’  data element must be maintained in its original state
Any modification, alteration , or update  to this data element is  strictly prohibited
across all intermediary  systems and stages  of data processing
